text.b: SCOPE OF JOB FOR LSTK CONTRACT FOR CDU3 HEATER F-101 REVAMP

page.text: This Job Specification along with its attachments broadly describes in detail the scope
of supply & work of the Contractor, execution methodology, applicable drawings
(existing and new) and standards / specifications applicable for the revamp of the
existing CDU3 Heater F-101 at BPCL MR
BPCL-MR intends to revamp the Crude Heater (CDU-3) with Tag No. 131-F-101 as
per the Study Report (Doc No. C128-00-80-45-SR-01 Rev. 0)
The revamp is intended to improve the existing heater absorb duty by addition of tubes
in radiant section, addition of future rows in convection section and burner gun
replacement.
In brief, the purpose of the revamp is: -
Increase the number of tubes per pass in the radiant section by installing 8 new 6”
radiant tubes per pass including associated modifications in the radiant coils,
crossovers, terminals, spigots, tube supports, radiant casing, floor, arch, and
refractory.
Add two rows of tubes (8 tubes per row) in the existing future tube rows of the
convection section including associated modifications in the convection section,
convection coils, and convection terminals.
Replace the existing Cast & Glass type APH with the latest design CAPH and GAPH
to enhance thermal duty and improve operational reliability including modification of
the APH support structure.
Replace all 24 existing oil guns with new oil guns.
Provide 24 additional skin thermocouples along with other required instrumentation-
related modifications.
The Contractor's scope includes Project management, execution, residual
engineering, detailing, fabrication drawing preparation, procurement, pre-fabrication /
fabrication (including supply of materials except for free issue materials as covered in
tender, consumables / items, as required), inspection, assembly, site erection and
testing assistance during pre-commissioning & commissioning etc. for the following
Fired Heater.
(Revamp)
CDU3
Heater
Existing Multicellular box type
Vertical tube heater with dual fired
burners and having balanced draft
system (Outboard Cast and glass
type APH with two FD fans and
one ID fan). Shares a Common
structure mounted stack with the
adjoining VDU heater
(Existing)
(Target duty)
P5
Page
of
The existing heater is a Multicellular box type Vertical tube heater. There is total of 272
tubes in the radiant section distributed in 8 fluid passes and 4 cells (without any wall
partition). The convection section is located on top of the radiant section.
Each radiant cell has 6 floor mounted dual fired (Fuel oil, Fuel gas and off-gas) burners,
i.e. total 24 burners in 4 cells, arranged in a staggered fashion.
The convection section for all the 8 passes is common and placed on top of the radiant
firebox.
The heater has a balanced draft system (Outboard Cast and glass type APH with two
FD fans and one ID fan). The heater shares a common structure mounted stack with
the adjoining VDU heater.
SITE LOCATION for the subject contract shall be CDU Heater Area in CDU3 Complex,
at BPCL Mumbai Refinery, Mahul, Mumbai 400071
The current heater revamp is based on Study Report (Doc No. C128-00-80-45-SR-01
Rev. 0) and list of attachment of tender index (CDU 3 EIL Tender Doc Annex) along
with the standards and specification and drawings thereof.
Bidders, after studying the contents of this package may decide to obtain clarifications
before making their offer. Any ambiguity shall be discussed at the pre-bid stage and
once having done so, the offer shall be in strict compliance to the requirements and
technical specifications of this package.
It shall be entirely the vendor’s responsibility to ensure compliance to codes and
standards specified for design, fabrication, testing etc. and to all requirements of the
requisition including its specifications and drawings after the order is placed

text.b: SCOPE OF WORK AND TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

page.text: For details on the following, please refer to EIL document
Scope of Supply and Work
Applicable drawings, codes, standards & specifications
Execution
Spares
Guarantee
Rejection
Quality Requirement
Vendor Data Requirement
Vendor’s battery limit shall be limited to the works on the heater radiant
, convection
section, APH section and all jobs associated with the same.
Page
of
All jobs on the radiant and convection coil till the heater inlet and outlet battery limit
including the inlet outlet header modification.
All platforms and structures outside the radiant box, convection section and APH
section in the respective elevation needing modification as part of the heater revamp.
Heater Stack is not part of the scope in this tender
Vendor shall commence the pre-commissioning activities for systems and equipment
after successful installation/erection as per the BPCL approved checklists and plans.
Assistance as required during check listing of revamped heater system, pre-commissioning
and commissioning shall be provided by the Contractor and shall be included in the lump
sum price
Housekeeping shall be carried out at fabrication yard and heater area during the pre-
shutdown and shutdown periods after completion of the work.
The bidders shall be evaluated technically based on the technical specifications,
Documents submitted along with Bid for evaluation and conditions mentioned in the
scope of supply/work.

text.b: The bidders shall be evaluated on overall lowest quote (L1) basis.

page.text: As this is NIL deviation tender, Bids submitted without/insufficient documents
mentioned above for bid evaluation are liable for rejection without any further
correspondence in this regard. Bidder are requested to furnish all the documents along
with Bid. Also, all the queries shall be cleared prior to submitting proposal and offers
with deviation are liable for rejection without any further correspondence.
HSE is the primary commitment of BPCL. Design studies, material supply and
construction activities must be delivered to comply with all HSE aspects of the project.
Vendor shall demonstrate his own HSE commitment.
Vendor shall deploy adequate safety officers during the pre-shutdown and shutdown
periods to ensure enhanced safety compliance.
Vendor must ensure that the design, fabrication and testing of his equipment doesn’t
endanger the health and security of his own employees and future operators of the
plant. In addition, all possible efforts must be made to minimize the environmental
impact of his activities.
All required precautions for the work to proceed safely without interruption (health,
safety, and environment) during site erection must be taken into account.
Vendor shall provide any necessary recommendation for installation in order to ensure
a safe erection and operation of his equipment.
Since during pre-shut down (PSD) the revamp activities are to be taken up in a running
plant, all necessary barricading, safety related precautions, tools & tackles shall be the
Contractor’s responsibility. All the OWNER’s safety recommendations, HSE requi
rements
and all applicable rules & regulations shall be complied to by the Contractor.
Page
of
Contractor to note that during Pre shut down hot work shall be permitted with compliance
of safety requirements / local barricading isolation. Owner’s approval for the same shall be
obtained before proceeding for hot work
All the vendor employees deputed at site, including supervisors, are required to attend
safety training in BPCL failing which they will not be accepted as supervisors
irrespective of their academic qualifications and work experience. All safety
precautions as per permit conditions and as per instructions from site in charge shall
be abided by the contractor.
It shall be the sole responsibility of the vendor for safe and proper execution of the
work. Specifying any procedure or approving any procedure by BPCL shall not relieve
the contractor from his responsibility towards fulfilling job objective.
All the BPCL's fire & safety rules and regulations or any other framed from time to time
are essentially to be followed and the vendor is liable for any lapses and penal
deduction from the bills shall be levied as per BPCL's safety financial deterrents.
The vendor shall compulsorily follow all the requirements/instructions of BPCL's work
authorization & permit systems and gate entry procedure for labour, staff & material as
per the specific procedures & regulations.
The bid shall include the following technical information along with the offer:
a)
Job execution philosophy including pre shutdown and shutdown activities and the
time schedule,
b)
Duly filled concession/deviation (as per attached) form concerning deviation and
exceptions if any
c)
Supplier HSE commitment
d)
List of references (Past track record as required for BQC)
m)
Other documents as listed in vendor data requirement.
As mentioned in RFQ.

text.b: 13.0 PRICE REDUCTION SCHEDULE (PRS) FOR DELAY IN DELIVERY /

page.text: As mentioned in RFQ.
As mentioned in RFQ.
Bidder has to quote based on SOR items only. Bidder must carry out the services as
well as has to supply the materials and other consumables as per scope anywhere
written in this tender.

of

text.b: SOR Jobs for heater miscellaneous works

page.text: LS
Bidder/contractor to attend the pre-bid meeting to get clear idea of the scope of work
and to clear queries if any.
The Contractor is advised to visit the site & ascertain the existing conditions of the VDU
heater. The contractor is also advised to get acquainted with site conditions, drawing,
specifications and other documents which form part of contract to be entered into. No
claim shall be entertained later, on the grounds of lack of knowledge
